ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-02-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Shu for (trustee) Thomas Lucas.

The Court stated that this is the 5th report and account, that the issue is whether the time put in by the trustee justifies allowing the guideline fee to be paid, that there are concerns regarding the lump sum entries and that there are 14 hours included from the prior accounting period which were already paid. Mr. Shu responded that in regards to the 14 hours it was for the accounting period of 9/11 through 10/13 and the preparation that happened after, that services were provided during this accounting period, that this accounting is for 27 months plus an additional 8 months, that the $9541.00 is for 34 months, that in regards to the issues of the all cash accounting there had been investments that had been liquidated, that Mr. Lucas had managed the assets for some time, that the hours for the accounting were performed by Mr. Lucas and that the November/December issue is a typo. The Court took the accounting petition under submission

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Jan-06-2016 Continued To Feb-17-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kevin Lachona for (petitioner) Thomas Lucas.

Mr. Lachona reported that the accounting is complete, that to close the trust they will need to bring the accounting up to date, would like to be able to file one final accounting to save costs and requested an 8 week continuance. The Court asked what was left to account. Mr. Lachona stated that this accounting is through 9/30/15 and that they need to account through December and that they could file the accounting report now but would then need to file another. The Court stated that it is not sure why it does not make sense to file it now, that it is now past due and that they can then complete the final accounting. Mr. Lachona stated that if they file it now it will not be final. The Court stated that it would be the interim accounting that was to be filed over a month ago. Mr. Lachona stated that they are trying to save fees for the estate. The status on filing an accounting was continued to February 17, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and counsel was told that if the final accounting is not complete at that time they are to file the interim accounting.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Siu. (Not Reported)
